# Per-user activity share of grand total from a delimited field

**Dataset**: `gx_08_feed`

Decodes a pipe-delimited `combo` field (`user|host|action|count`) and ranks each user by their share of total activity — the relative-contribution view that tells an analyst which identity dominates a noisy feed. It `split`s the composite string, pulls positional parts with `arrayindex`, coerces the count with `to_number`, aggregates per user with `comp`, then uses `windowcomp sum(user_total)` (no `by`) to fold a grand total onto every row so `divide`/`multiply` can yield a percentage. Gotcha embodied: a `windowcomp` with no `by` computes a single dataset-wide aggregate broadcast to all rows — exactly how you get a denominator for per-group percentages; remember `arrayindex` is zero-based.

```sql
dataset = gx_08_feed
| alter parts = split(combo, "|")
| alter user = arrayindex(parts, 0), cnt = to_number(arrayindex(parts, 3))
| comp sum(cnt) as user_total, count() as user_events by user
| windowcomp sum(user_total) as grand_total
| alter pct = multiply(divide(user_total, grand_total), 100)
| sort desc user_total
| fields user, user_events, user_total, grand_total, pct
```
